pattern: Collapsing a subarray to its maximum means the kept values must be non-decreasing group maxima, so greedily closing each group the instant its running max reaches the previous group's value keeps the most pieces.
hints: Each surviving value is the maximum of a contiguous block, and reading those block maxima left to right they must be non-decreasing.
To keep as many blocks as possible, every block should be as short as it can while still being at least as large as the block before it.
Sweep left to right tracking the value of the last completed block; grow the current block only until its running maximum reaches that value, then close it and start the next one.

edge_inputs.description: dip absorbed to keep three groups
a large early value forces merging
leading peak forces a single group

stress.description: random small-range values so many groupings compete, sized for the polynomial oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 12) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maximumPossibleSize(self, nums):
        floor = 0
        count = 0
        cur = 0
        started = False
        for x in nums:
            cur = max(cur, x) if started else x
            started = True
            if cur >= floor:
                count += 1
                floor = cur
                started = False
                cur = 0
        return count
